commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f38dba70fc056af6ebabbdf6085f5b1f2aa9aa8e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes reply-turn admission to time out and reclaim already-aborted active reply operations, with regression tests for superseded and healthy active runs.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +84. Total +98 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: dispatch-created reply operations call `retainFailureUntilComplete()`, and registry code plus tests show `fail()` alone leaves retained failures active. I did not run a live Telegram or provider scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The retained-operation defect is concrete, but missing contributor real behavior proof makes this a human-only merge blocker rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: reclaim superseded reply turns
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

A visible reply turn can wait forever behind an active reply operation that has already been aborted by a superseding turn. That leaves the per-session reply lane occupied until process restart clears the in-memory registry, even though the older run is no longer healthy work.

## Summary

- bound visible-turn waits when the active reply operation has already been aborted/superseded
- reclaim the dead per-session reply lane after the existing settle timeout
- keep healthy active reply runs protected behind the existing indefinite wait behaviour

## Evidence

- Added a regression test where an already-aborted active reply operation is reclaimed by a newer visible turn instead of wedging.
- Added a guard test proving healthy active runs are not reclaimed before completion.
- Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.test.ts` locally: 12/12 passed.
- Ran `oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.test.ts`.
- Ran `git diff --check`.

## Notes

The change keeps the existing indefinite wait contract for healthy active runs. Only active operations whose abort signal has already fired receive the bounded settle wait and reclaim path.
